title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Designed RNA-seq workflows, JW26ADS8192 provides streamlined pipeline differential expression results. Use SummarizedExperiment object R package interface, supply raw count matrices sample metadata (TSV/CSV) directly via command-line interface.

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
code: Rscript -e "Rapp::install_pkg_cli_apps('JW26ADS8192')"

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example
what: Step 2: Load example dataset and set paths
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
code: ex_counts_path <- system.file("testdata", "example_counts.tsv", package = "JW26ADS8192") ex_meta_path   <- system.file("testdata", "example_meta.tsv", package = "JW26ADS8192")  Sys.setenv(EX_COUNTS = ex_counts_path) Sys.setenv(EX_META   = ex_meta_path)

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
what: Step 3.1: Determine the Low Expression Filter Threshold
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Evaluate model performance across different threshold values select best one. Results output filtering_analysis.tsv table results folder. Success confirmation read: “Saved RDS. Done.”
code: JW26ADS8192 determine_filter_threshold --count $EX_COUNTS --meta $EX_META --output ./results/

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Using threshold value determined Step 1, manually replace min_count_per_gene variable filter. Default min_count_per_gene = 10. Success confirmation read: “Saved RDS. Done.” Output se_filtered.rds save “./results/”
code: JW26ADS8192 filter_low_exp_genes --count $EX_COUNTS --meta $EX_META --output ./results/

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
what: Step 3.3: Differential Gene Expression Analysis via DESeq2
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: remaining filtered genes, preform DESeq2 analyze gene expression. Replace group_var column categorized. Default group_var = “cell-type”. Replace ref_level specific cell type reference. Default re_level = “Tconv” Success confirmation read: “Saved RDS. Done.” Output se_dge.rds save “./results/”
code: JW26ADS8192 run_DESeq2 --input ./results/se_filtered.rds --output ./results/

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
what: Step 3.4: Apply log2_shrinkage on DESeq2 results to improve estimates.
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Replace shrinkage appropriate GLM estimator. Default shrinkage = “apeglm” Success confirmation read: “Saved RDS. Done.” Results output dge_shrink.rds dge_shrink.tsv table saved “./results/”
code: JW26ADS8192 log2_shrinkage  --input ./results/se_dge.rds --output ./results/

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Summarize non-significant regulated genes data set. Replace p_threshold appropriate adjusted p-value threshold. Default p_threshold = 0.05. Replace fc_threshold appropriate fold-change threshold. Default fc_threshold = 0.5. Success confirmation read: “Saved RDS. Done.” Results output gene_reg_summary.rds gene_reg_summary.tsv table saved “./results/”
code: JW26ADS8192 gene_regulation_summary  --input ./results/dge_shrink.rds --output ./results/

previous_headings: Analysis via Command-Line Interface (Rapp): Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Generate volcano plot visualize gene_reg_summary results. Use p_threshold fc_threshold values utilized Step 5. Replace set_title correct title. Default set_title = “Volcano Plot - Lymph Node Treg vs Tconv”. Replace xlab correct x-axis title. Default xlab = “log2 Fold Change (Treg vs Tconv)”. Success confirmation read: “Saved RDS. Done.” Results output volcano_plot.rds, volcano_plot.pdf, volcano_plot.png saved “./results/”
code: JW26ADS8192 generate_volcano --input ./results/dge_shrink.rds --output ./results/

what: Step 3.1: Determine the Low Expression Filter Threshold
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Evaluate model performance across different threshold values select best one.Replace group_var column categorized. Default group_var = “cell-type”. Replace ref_level specific cell type reference. Default re_level = “Tconv”. Replace assay_name appropriate label. Default `assay_name = “counts”.
code: example_se_filtering_assessment <- determine_filter_threshold(   se_ln             = example_se,   count_thresholds  = c(0, 1, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500),   assay_name        = "counts",   ref_level         = "Tconv",   group_var         = "cell_type",   p_threshold       = 0.05   )

title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Using threshold value determined Step 1, manually replace min_count_per_gene variable filter. Use SE utilized Step 1. Default min_count_per_gene = 10. Replace assay_name appropriate label. Default `assay_name = “counts”.
code: se_filtered <- filter_low_exp_genes(   se_ln               = example_se,   min_count_per_group = 10,   assay_name          = "counts"   )

previous_headings: Analysis via R Studio: Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
what: Step 3.3: Differential Gene Expression Analysis via DESeq2
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: remaining filtered genes, preform DESeq2 analyze gene expression. Replace group_var column categorized. Default group_var = “cell-type”. Replace ref_level specific cell type reference. Default re_level = “Tconv”
code: se_dge <- run_DESeq2(   se_ln     = se_filtered,    group_var = "cell_type",    ref_level = "Tconv"   )

previous_headings: Analysis via R Studio: Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
what: Step 3.4: Apply log2_shrinkage on DESeq2 results to improve estimates.
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Replace shrinkage appropriate GLM estimator. Default shrinkage = “apeglm”.
code: se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(   dds       = se_dge,    shrinkage = "apeglm"   )

previous_headings: Analysis via R Studio: Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Summarize non-significant regulated genes data set. Replace p_threshold appropriate adjusted p-value threshold. Default p_threshold = 0.05. Replace fc_threshold appropriate fold-change threshold. Default fc_threshold = 0.5.
code: DESeq2_gene_reg_summary <- gene_regulation_summary(   res_df       = se_dge_shrink,   p_threshold  = 0.05,   fc_threshold = 0.5   )

previous_headings: Analysis via R Studio: Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Generate volcano plot visualize gene_reg_summary results. Use p_threshold fc_threshold values utilized Step 5. Replace set_title correct title. Default set_title = “Volcano Plot - Lymph Node Treg vs Tconv”. Replace xlab correct x-axis title. Default xlab = “log2 Fold Change (Treg vs Tconv)”.
code: example_se_volcano <- generate_volcano(   res_df       = se_dge_shrink,   fc_threshold = 0.5,   xlab         = "log2 Fold Change (Treg vs Tconv)",   set_title    = "Volcano Plot - Lymph Node Treg vs Tconv",   p_threshold  = 0.05   )

previous_headings: Analysis via R Studio: Quick Example > Step 3: Start the Differential Gene Expression Analysis
title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: Export se_dge_shrink, DESeq2_gene_reg_summary, example_se_filtering_assessment data frames TSV tables volcano plots PDF PNG images. Outputs export “de_output” current working directory unless explicitly indicated.
code: example_se_exports<- export_outputs(   res_df         = se_dge_shrink,   summary_df     = DESeq2_gene_reg_summary,   filtering_diag = example_se_filtering_assessment,   volcano        = example_se_volcano,   output_dir     = file.path(tempdir(), "de_output")    )

title: Getting Started with JW26ADS8192
text: JW26ADS8192 R-package includes additional function (7 total functions) collectively generates exports filtering_analysis.tsv, dge_shrink.tsv, volcano_plot.pdf, volcano_plot.png current working directory. JW26ADS8192 CLI includes 6 functions, generate export deliverable within single step.

title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
text: Designed RNA-seq workflows, JW26ADS8192 provides streamlined pipeline differential expression results. Use SummarizedExperiment object R package interface, supply raw count matrices sample metadata (TSV/CSV) directly via command-line interface.

previous_headings: R Studio Analysis
title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
code: # Install the package from GitHub remotes::install_github("JW26ADS8192")

previous_headings: R Studio Analysis
title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
code: # Load package and example data (SummarizedExperiment) library(JW26ADS8192) data(example_se)  # Pick the optimal 'minimun gene count' filtering threshold. example_se_filtering_assessment <- determine_filter_threshold(   se_ln            = example_se,   count_thresholds = c(0, 1, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500),    assay_name       = "counts",    ref_level        = "Tconv",    group_var        = "cell_type",    p_threshold      = 0.05   )  # Filter out the low expression genes se_filtered <- filter_low_exp_genes(   se_ln               = example_se,    min_count_per_group = 10,    assay_name          = "counts"   )  # Run the DESeq2 pipeline se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_ln = se_filtered, group_var = "cell_type", ref_level = "Tconv")  # Shrink log2 fold-change estimates se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(dds = se_dge, shrinkage = "apeglm")  # Summarize Gene Expression DESeq2_gene_reg_summary<- gene_regulation_summary(   res_df       = se_dge_shrink,    p_threshold  = 0.05,    fc_threshold =  0.5   )  # Visualize example_se_volcano<- generate_volcano(   res_df        = se_dge_shrink,    fc_threshold  =  0.5,    xlab          = "log2 Fold Change (Treg vs Tconv)",    set_title     = "Volcano Plot - Lymph Node Treg vs Tconv",    p_threshold   = 0.05   )  # Export Results example_se_exports<- export_outputs(   res_df         = se_dge_shrink,    summary_df     = DESeq2_gene_reg_summary,    filtering_diag = example_se_filtering_assessment,    volcano        = example_se_volcano,    output_dir     = file.path("(tempdir()", "de_output") )

previous_headings: Command-Line Interface (via Rapp)
title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
code: # Install the package from GitHub Rscript -e "Rapp::install_pkg_cli_apps('JW26ADS8192')"

previous_headings: Command-Line Interface (via Rapp)
title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
code: # Load package and example data (TSV) ex_counts_path <- system.file("testdata", "example_counts.tsv", package = "JW26ADS8192") ex_meta_path   <- system.file("testdata", "example_meta.tsv", package = "JW26ADS8192")  Sys.setenv(EX_COUNTS = ex_counts_path) Sys.setenv(EX_META   = ex_meta_path)  # Pick the optimal 'minimun gene count' filtering threshold. JW26ADS8192 determine_filter_threshold --count $EX_COUNTS --meta $EX_META --output ./results/  # Filter out the low expression genes JW26ADS8192 filter_low_exp_genes --count $EX_COUNTS --meta $EX_META --output ./results/  # Run the DESeq2 pipeline JW26ADS8192 run_DESeq2 --input ./results/se_filtered.rds --output ./results/  # Shrink log2 fold-change estimates JW26ADS8192 log2_shrinkage  --input ./results/se_dge.rds --output ./results/  # Summarize Gene Expression JW26ADS8192 gene_regulation_summary  --input ./results/dge_shrink.rds --output ./results/  # Visualize JW26ADS8192 generate_volcano --input ./results/dge_shrink.rds --output ./results/

title: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2
text: JW26ADS8192 R-package includes additional function (7 total functions) collectively generates exports filtering_analysis.tsv, dge_shrink.tsv, volcano_plot.pdf, volcano_plot.png current working directory. JW26ADS8192 CLI includes 6 functions, generate export deliverable within single step.

what: JW26ADS8192: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2 — JW26ADS8192-package
title: JW26ADS8192: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2 — JW26ADS8192-package
text: Provides tool differential gene analysis lymph node regulatory T cells (Tregs) conventional T cells (Tconv) isolated four mouse tissues (fat, liver, lymph node, skin). Tool includes functions (1) filtering low expression gene counts, (2) preforms DESeq2 differential expression analysis, (3) applies log2fold-change shrinkage DESeq2 results, (4) summarizes number //non-significant genes given thresholds, (5) computes indepdenpt filtering diagonsitics, (6) visualizes results volcano plot, (7) exportsthe results TSV files.

title: JW26ADS8192: ADS 8192 Differential Expression with DESeq2 — JW26ADS8192-package
text: Maintainer: Wilson Jewel jewel.wilson@stjude.org (ORCID)

was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time.  # Step 2; Filter low expression genes se_filtered<- filter_low_exp_genes(example_se, min_count_per_group = 10) #> Genes after filtering: 50  #> colData names: cell_id cell_type batch  # Step 3: Run the DESeq2 pipeline to get differential gene expression results  se_dge<- run_DESeq2(se_filtered) #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> -- note: fitType='parametric', but the dispersion trend was not well captured by the #>    function: y = a/x + b, and a local regression fit was automatically substituted. #>    specify fitType='local' or 'mean' to avoid this message next time. #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing  # Step 4:Run log2_shrinkage on DESeq2 results to improve estimates se_dge_shrink <- log2_shrinkage(se_dge) #> using 'apeglm' for LFC shrinkage. If used in published research, please cite: #>     Zhu, A., Ibrahim, J.G., Love, M.I. (2018) Heavy-tailed prior distributions for #>     sequence count data: removing the noise and preserving large differences. #>     Bioinformatics. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bty895  # Step 5:Generate a volcano plot from log2_shrinkage results example_se_volcano<- generate_volcano(se_dge_shrink)  # Step 6: Generate the regulation summary of the genes DESeq2_gene_reg_summary<- gene_regulation_summary(se_dge_shrink)  # Step 7: Export final results example_se_exports<- export_outputs( res_df = se_dge_shrink, summary_df = DESeq2_gene_reg_summary, filtering_diag = example_se_filtering_assessment, volcano = example_se_volcano, output_dir = file.path(tempdir(), "de_output") ) #> Export complete. Files saved: #> /tmp/Rtmp3dhKRS/de_output/de_results.tsv #> /tmp/Rtmp3dhKRS/de_output/de_summary.tsv #> /tmp/Rtmp3dhKRS/de_output/filtering_diagnostics.tsv #> /tmp/Rtmp3dhKRS/de_output/volcano_plot.pdf #> /tmp/Rtmp3dhKRS/de_output/volcano_plot.png
